title: Aluminum Flat Cut Letters | Wholesale | Sunlite
metaDescription: Wholesale aluminum flat cut letters precision-routed from solid plate. Brushed, painted, or anodized finishes. German-engineered quality, 3-week sign trade delivery.
heroSubtitle: Precision CNC-routed from solid aluminum plate, finished to architectural standards, and ready for effortless installation. The industry workhorse for dimensional lettering.

sections.heading: Why Aluminum Dominates the Flat Cut Letter Market
sections.content:
Aluminum flat cut letters account for the majority of dimensional lettering projects, and for good reason. The material combines natural corrosion resistance, excellent machinability, and a professional appearance that works across virtually every commercial application. From corporate lobbies to exterior building identification, aluminum flat cut letters deliver a refined, dimensional look at a price point that fits most project budgets.

At Sunlite Signs, we route our aluminum flat cut letters from solid 6061-T6 plate using high-speed CNC routers with carbide tooling. This process produces clean, burr-free edges and sharp inside corners that cheaper laser-cut alternatives simply cannot match. The difference is visible in the finished product — clean edges, tight radius corners, and a surface free of heat discoloration.

Our wholesale-only model means your sign shop gets these premium letters at trade pricing. We handle the precision manufacturing and finishing; you handle the client relationship and installation. It's a partnership built on the principle that each party should focus on what they do best.

sections.heading: Finishing Options & Specifications
sections.content:
Raw aluminum is just the starting point. The finish is what gives flat cut letters their character, and Sunlite Signs offers a comprehensive range of finishing options:

  • Brushed Aluminum: Directional satin finish applied with consistent grain pattern. Available in natural aluminum, gold-tone, or black anodized
  • Painted: Automotive-grade polyurethane in any PMS, RAL, or custom color. Multiple sheens available from matte to high-gloss
  • Anodized: Architectural anodizing in clear, bronze, black, or champagne. Extremely durable and resistant to fingerprints and smudging
  • Polished: Mirror-finish polishing for a high-end reflective appearance. Available with clear-coat protection to slow oxidation

Standard letter thickness ranges from 1/8" to 1" solid plate. For letters taller than 12", we recommend 3/8" or 1/2" thickness to provide adequate visual presence on the wall. We provide stud-mount hardware with every order — just specify your mounting substrate (drywall, masonry, glass, or wood) and we'll include the appropriate fastening system.

All flat cut aluminum letters ship with a paper template and installation guide that makes precise spacing and alignment straightforward for your install crew.

sections.heading: Applications Across Commercial Sectors
sections.content:
Aluminum flat cut letters serve an extraordinarily wide range of applications. Here are the most common scenarios we produce for through our sign shop partners:

  • Corporate Lobbies: Painted or brushed letters mounted to feature walls behind reception desks. Typically 4" to 12" tall in the company's brand colors.
  • Building Identification: Exterior-grade letters mounted to building facades for street-level identification. Usually 12" to 36" tall with pin-mount or stud-mount installation.
  • Wayfinding: Smaller-scale letters (2" to 6") for interior directional signs, room identification, and floor-level indicators.
  • Retail Storefronts: Brand name lettering for shop facades, often combined with a backer panel or raceway for a clean, unified look.

Sunlite Signs produces flat cut aluminum letters for all of these applications under one roof. Whether your project calls for three letters or three hundred, our CNC production system delivers consistent quality and fast turnaround. Standard lead time is three weeks from approved artwork, with rush options available for projects on tight schedules.

faqs.question: What is the maximum letter size you can produce?
faqs.answer: Our CNC router bed handles letters up to 48" tall from a single piece of aluminum plate. Larger letters can be produced in sections with concealed splice joints. There's no practical limit to the letter size we can manufacture.

faqs.question: What thickness should I specify for flat cut aluminum letters?
faqs.answer: For interior letters under 12" tall, 1/4" thickness is standard. For exterior letters or letters over 12", we recommend 3/8" to 1/2" for adequate visual presence. Letters over 24" typically look best at 3/4" or 1" thickness.

faqs.question: How are flat cut letters mounted to the wall?
faqs.answer: We provide threaded stud mounts on the back of each letter. Letters are installed by drilling holes per our paper template, inserting the studs, and securing with adhesive or mechanical fasteners. We include hardware appropriate for your specified wall substrate.

faqs.question: Can you match a specific PMS color on painted letters?
faqs.answer: Yes. We use a spectrophotometer-matched paint system that achieves exact PMS and RAL color matches. We can provide painted samples for approval on color-critical projects before production begins.

title: Stainless Steel Flat Cut Letters | Wholesale
metaDescription: Wholesale stainless steel flat cut letters in 304 or 316 grade. Brushed, polished, or painted finishes. German-engineered precision, 3-week delivery for sign shops.
h1: Wholesale Stainless Steel Flat Cut Letters
heroSubtitle: Premium dimensional lettering crafted from solid stainless steel plate. Brushed, mirror-polished, or painted to elevate any commercial environment.

sections.heading: The Premium Choice in Dimensional Lettering
sections.content:
Stainless steel flat cut letters occupy the premium tier of dimensional signage, delivering a level of material quality and visual sophistication that aluminum and acrylic simply cannot replicate. The natural luster of stainless steel communicates permanence, precision, and prestige — qualities that resonate strongly in corporate, financial, healthcare, and luxury retail environments.

Sunlite Signs fabricates stainless steel flat cut letters from solid 304-grade plate as standard, with 316-grade available for coastal and industrial installations where additional corrosion resistance is required. Our waterjet and CNC routing processes produce clean, precise cuts without the heat-affected zones that laser cutting leaves behind. The result is a letter with pristine edges that require minimal finishing.

For sign shops serving high-end commercial clients, stainless steel flat cut letters represent both a quality upgrade and a margin opportunity. The material cost is higher than aluminum, but the perceived value to the end client is disproportionately higher — allowing your shop to command premium pricing that reflects the product's true quality.

sections.heading: Finish Options for Stainless Steel Letters
sections.content:
The beauty of stainless steel lies in the variety of finishes available from a single base material. Sunlite Signs offers the following standard finish options:

  • No. 4 Brushed: The most popular stainless finish for signage. A uniform, directional satin brush produces a sophisticated look that hides fingerprints and minor surface marks. Available in standard grain or custom patterns.
  • Mirror Polished: A highly reflective finish achieved through progressive mechanical polishing. Creates dramatic visual impact and reflects surrounding architecture. Best suited for interior applications where surface scratching is minimal.
  • Bead Blasted: A non-directional matte finish that provides a soft, contemporary appearance. Excellent for modern architectural environments.
  • Painted: Stainless steel letters can be painted in any color using our standard polyurethane process. This combines stainless durability with unlimited color flexibility.
  • PVD Coated: Physical Vapor Deposition coating — a premium option also available on lobby sign letters in gold, rose gold, bronze, or black titanium tones. Creates a metallic color appearance that is extremely durable and scratch-resistant.

Our engineering team specifies the right finish for each project. Every stainless steel letter receives a protective film during manufacturing that remains in place through shipping. This ensures the finish arrives at your shop in pristine condition, ready for installation.

sections.heading: Engineering & Installation Considerations
sections.content:
Stainless steel is significantly denser than aluminum — roughly 2.5 times heavier by volume. This weight difference affects both the mounting system design and the installation process, and Sunlite Signs accounts for these factors in every project:

  • Mounting Hardware: We use heavier-gauge threaded studs and specify stronger wall anchors than we would for equivalent aluminum letters. Every order ships with substrate-specific mounting hardware.
  • Template Precision: Heavier letters have less tolerance for misaligned mounting holes. Our paper templates are CNC-plotted for exact positioning, and we include level reference lines for multi-line layouts.
  • Handling: Large stainless letters require careful handling to avoid surface damage during installation. We provide handling guidelines and recommend padded gloves for the install crew.

For exterior installations, stainless steel letters are virtually maintenance-free. The material's inherent corrosion resistance means no repainting, no clear-coat touch-ups, and no rust staining on the building facade. This durability makes stainless an excellent long-term value proposition despite the higher upfront cost.

Standard lead time for stainless steel flat cut letters is three weeks from approved artwork. PVD-coated letters may require an additional week depending on the coating facility's schedule.

faqs.question: What grade of stainless steel do you use?
faqs.answer: We use 304-grade stainless steel as standard, which provides excellent corrosion resistance for most environments. For coastal installations or areas with high chemical exposure, we offer 316-grade stainless for enhanced corrosion protection.

faqs.question: Will stainless steel letters rust or tarnish over time?
faqs.answer: No. Stainless steel is inherently corrosion-resistant and will not rust or tarnish under normal conditions. Brushed finishes maintain their appearance for decades with occasional cleaning. Mirror-polished finishes may develop a light patina over time in exterior applications.

faqs.question: What is the maximum thickness for stainless steel flat cut letters?
faqs.answer: We can produce stainless steel flat cut letters up to 3/4" thick from solid plate. For greater depth, we fabricate return-style letters with flat-cut stainless faces and welded returns.

faqs.question: How do stainless steel letters compare in cost to aluminum?
faqs.answer: Stainless steel flat cut letters typically cost 2-3 times more than equivalent aluminum letters, depending on size, thickness, and finish. The premium is justified by the material's superior durability, prestige, and zero-maintenance exterior performance.

faqs.question: Can you do gold or rose gold finishes on stainless letters?
faqs.answer: Yes. Our PVD (Physical Vapor Deposition) coating process produces gold, rose gold, bronze, and black titanium finishes that are extremely durable and scratch-resistant. PVD coatings are far more durable than plating or paint for metallic color effects.

title: Acrylic Flat Cut Letters | Wholesale | Sunlite
metaDescription: Wholesale acrylic flat cut letters laser-cut from premium cast acrylic sheet. Wide color range, translucent options available. German precision, 3-week trade delivery.
heroSubtitle: Vibrant colors, clean edges, and creative versatility. Laser-cut from premium cast acrylic in dozens of standard colors and custom options.

sections.heading: Acrylic Flat Cut Letters: Versatile and Cost-Effective
sections.content:
Acrylic flat cut letters offer a unique combination of vibrant color options, creative versatility, and budget-friendly pricing that makes them a staple in the sign industry. Unlike metal letters that are limited to paint or natural metal finishes, acrylic letters come in dozens of through-colored options — the color goes all the way through the material, so edge chips and scratches don't reveal a different color underneath.

Sunlite Signs laser-cuts our acrylic flat cut letters from premium cast acrylic sheet, not extruded. Cast acrylic machines cleaner, offers better optical clarity, and delivers more consistent color than its extruded counterpart. Our CO2 laser cutting process produces polished, flame-finished edges directly from the machine, eliminating the need for secondary edge finishing on most applications.

For sign shops, acrylic flat cut letters are a high-margin product with broad appeal. They're fast to produce, lightweight enough for adhesive mounting in many cases, and available in an almost unlimited range of colors and effects. From corporate lobbies to retail environments, acrylic letters deliver a professional dimensional look without the weight and cost of metal.

sections.heading: Color Options & Material Specifications
sections.content:
We stock a comprehensive range of acrylic colors and effects for flat cut letter production:

  • Opaque Solids: Over 40 standard colors from major acrylic manufacturers including Plaskolite, Evonik, and Altuglas. Includes whites, blacks, primaries, and specialty colors.
  • Translucent Colors: Ideal for halo-lit or backlit letter applications. Light passes through the material for a glowing effect when paired with LED illumination behind the letter.
  • Clear & Frosted: Crystal-clear acrylic for a glass-like look, or frosted finishes for a softer, diffused appearance. Popular for modern interior applications.
  • Metallic & Special Effects: Mirror acrylic, fluorescent colors, and specialty sheets like marble-effect and wood-grain acrylic.

Standard thicknesses range from 1/8" to 1" in most colors. We recommend 1/4" to 3/8" for typical signage applications, which provides good visual depth while keeping weight manageable. For letters over 18" tall, we suggest 1/2" or 3/4" to maintain proportional depth.

Every acrylic letter ships with a protective masking film on both faces that remains in place through shipping and installation. This prevents surface scratching during handling and ensures the letters arrive in perfect condition.

sections.heading: Installation Methods for Acrylic Letters
sections.content:
Acrylic flat cut letters are significantly lighter than their metal counterparts, which opens up installation options that aren't practical with heavier materials:

  • Stud Mount: Threaded studs bonded to the back of each letter with industrial adhesive. This is the standard method for most wall substrates and provides a dimensional standoff from the mounting surface.
  • Adhesive Mount: VHB (Very High Bond) tape applied to the back of each letter for direct-to-surface mounting. Ideal for smooth surfaces like glass, laminate, and painted drywall where drilling is not desired.
  • Spacer Mount: Aluminum or acrylic spacers between the letter and wall for an elevated floating effect with a shadow gap beneath.
  • Raceway Mount: Letters mounted to a painted aluminum raceway for clean, wire-free exterior installations.

Sunlite Signs includes your specified mounting hardware with every acrylic letter order. We also provide a CNC-plotted paper template that shows exact letter positions, spacing, and level lines for precise installation. For multi-line layouts, the template includes reference points that keep everything aligned across the entire composition.

Standard production time for acrylic flat cut letters is two to three weeks from approved artwork. Simple color orders in stock materials may ship sooner.

faqs.question: Are acrylic letters suitable for outdoor use?
faqs.answer: Yes, with caveats. Cast acrylic has good UV resistance and will maintain its color and clarity outdoors. However, acrylic is more susceptible to impact damage than metal. For high-traffic exterior locations, we recommend metal letters or acrylic with a protective standoff to reduce impact risk.

faqs.question: Can acrylic letters be backlit?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. Translucent acrylic colors are specifically designed for backlit applications. Mount the letters with spacers and install LED strips or modules behind them for a dramatic halo or through-lit effect.

faqs.question: What is the smallest letter size you can produce in acrylic?
faqs.answer: We can laser-cut acrylic letters as small as 1" tall, depending on the font. Very thin stroke widths may require a minimum size of 1.5" to 2" for structural integrity. Send us your font and we'll advise on the minimum practical size.

faqs.question: Do acrylic letters yellow over time?
faqs.answer: High-quality cast acrylic has excellent UV stability and resists yellowing for many years. We exclusively use premium cast acrylic from major manufacturers, which carries significantly better long-term clarity than cheaper extruded alternatives.

title: Brass Flat Cut Letters | Wholesale | Sunlite Signs
metaDescription: Wholesale brass flat cut letters from solid plate. Polished, brushed, antiqued, or patina finishes. German-engineered craftsmanship, 3-week delivery for sign trade.
heroSubtitle: Solid brass dimensional letters that communicate heritage, craftsmanship, and prestige. Available in polished, brushed, antiqued, and natural patina finishes.

sections.heading: Brass Letters: Timeless Elegance for Premium Projects
sections.content:
Brass has been the material of choice for prestige signage for centuries, and its appeal shows no signs of diminishing. The warm, golden tone of brass communicates heritage, quality, and permanence in a way that no painted or coated material can replicate. For law firms, financial institutions, luxury hotels, universities, and historic properties, brass flat cut letters are the definitive choice for dimensional signage.

Sunlite Signs machines our brass flat cut letters from solid C360 free-machining brass plate — the same alloy used in architectural hardware and marine fittings. This material provides excellent machinability for clean edges, good corrosion resistance, and the rich golden color that defines the brass aesthetic.

Our German engineering approach ensures that every brass letter meets exacting dimensional tolerances and finish quality standards. We understand that brass projects often carry high expectations from both the sign shop and the end client, and we deliver a product that exceeds those expectations consistently.

sections.heading: Finish Options for Brass Flat Cut Letters
sections.content:
The versatility of brass finishes allows a single material to serve a remarkably wide range of design aesthetics:

  • Polished Bright: High-shine mirror finish that showcases the warm golden color of brass at its most vibrant. Typically protected with a lacquer or clear-coat to slow natural patina development.
  • Satin Brushed: A directional brush that softens the reflectivity while maintaining the warmth of the brass tone. Excellent for corporate environments where mirror-bright might be too flashy.
  • Antiqued: Chemical darkening that simulates decades of natural aging, producing rich brown tones in recessed areas while highlights retain the golden brass color. Perfect for historic buildings and traditional brand identities.
  • Natural Patina: Uncoated brass that will develop its own unique patina over time. The surface gradually shifts from bright gold to warm brown-green, creating a living finish that evolves with the building.
  • Oxidized Black: A deep black chemical finish with brass undertones visible at certain angles. Creates a dramatic, sophisticated appearance, complementing illuminated logo boxes in upscale settings.

For projects where the client wants the brass look — as opposed to stainless steel without the natural aging process, we apply a catalyzed lacquer clear-coat that preserves the as-delivered appearance for years. For clients who prefer the living patina, we leave the letters uncoated and include care instructions.

faqs.question: Will brass letters tarnish if left uncoated?
faqs.answer: Yes, uncoated brass will naturally develop a patina over time, shifting from bright gold toward brown and eventually greenish tones. Many clients consider this aging desirable. If the bright finish is preferred, we apply a protective lacquer that significantly slows the patina process.

faqs.question: How do brass letters compare in cost to stainless steel?
faqs.answer: Brass flat cut letters are typically 20-40% more expensive than equivalent stainless steel letters due to higher raw material costs. The premium is warranted for projects where the warm golden color of brass is integral to the design intent.

faqs.question: Can brass letters be used outdoors?
faqs.answer: Yes. Brass has excellent outdoor durability and is naturally resistant to corrosion. Exterior brass letters will develop a patina over time unless protected with a marine-grade lacquer. Many exterior projects specifically choose brass for the dignified patina it develops.

faqs.question: What thickness options are available for brass flat cut letters?
faqs.answer: We offer brass flat cut letters in thicknesses from 1/8" to 3/4" solid plate. For greater depth, we can fabricate built-up brass letters with flat-cut faces and metal returns. Standard thickness for most signage applications is 1/4" to 3/8".

faqs.question: Can you match a specific antique brass finish from a reference sample?
faqs.answer: Yes. Our finishing team can match antique brass tones from a physical reference sample or detailed photographs. We recommend providing a sample for color-critical projects and will produce test pieces for your approval before proceeding with the full order.

title: Brushed Metal Letters | Wholesale | Sunlite Signs
metaDescription: Wholesale brushed metal letters in aluminum, stainless steel, and brass. Consistent directional grain finishes. German-engineered, 3-week delivery for sign professionals.
heroSubtitle: Consistent directional satin finishes on aluminum, stainless steel, or brass substrates. The refined choice for corporate and architectural signage applications.

sections.heading: The Appeal of Brushed Metal Finishes in Signage
sections.content:
Brushed metal finishes have become the de facto standard for professional commercial signage, and the reasons are both aesthetic and practical. A directional satin brush softens the reflectivity of raw metal, reducing glare while maintaining the inherent beauty and material authenticity of the substrate. The result is a sophisticated, understated appearance that reads as premium without being ostentatious.

From a maintenance perspective, brushed finishes are far more practical than polished or mirror surfaces. The directional grain pattern conceals fingerprints, minor surface scratches, and environmental deposits that would be immediately visible on a polished surface. This makes brushed metal letters ideal for high-traffic environments like lobbies, elevators, and corridor wayfinding where the letters will be seen — and occasionally touched — by hundreds of people daily.

Sunlite Signs applies our brushed finishes using precision abrasive belt equipment that produces a perfectly uniform grain pattern across every letter in the set. Unlike hand-brushed finishes that vary in direction and intensity from letter to letter, our machine-applied process ensures that the grain direction, depth, and reflectivity are identical across every piece in the order — a consistency also found in our channel letter production.

sections.heading: Material Options for Brushed Letters
sections.content:
We produce brushed metal letters in three base materials, each with distinct characteristics:

  • Brushed Aluminum: The most popular option for brushed metal signage. Lightweight, affordable, and corrosion-resistant. Available in natural silver, anodized bronze, anodized black, and anodized champagne. Can also be clear-coated over the raw brushed finish for a warm, natural aluminum appearance.
  • Brushed Stainless Steel: Premium option with superior scratch resistance and a brighter, more reflective satin surface than aluminum. No. 4 finish is the industry standard. Ideal for high-end corporate and healthcare applications.
  • Brushed Brass: Warm golden satin finish for heritage and luxury environments. Available with lacquer protection to maintain the as-delivered appearance or uncoated for natural aging.

Grain direction is an important design consideration. Our standard orientation is horizontal grain (reading left to right), which is the most common specification for architectural signage. We also offer vertical grain and diagonal grain on request. For multi-line letter layouts, we ensure consistent grain direction across all lines for visual cohesion.

Every brushed metal letter order includes a finish sample chip upon request, allowing you and your client to approve the exact finish before full production begins. This eliminates surprises at installation and ensures everyone is aligned on expectations.

sections.heading: Specifying Brushed Metal Letters for Your Projects
sections.content:
When specifying brushed metal letters for a project, provide us with the following information for the fastest turnaround and most accurate quote:

  • Material: Aluminum, stainless steel, or brass
  • Finish Specification: Standard satin brush, fine brush, or coarse brush. Include anodize color for aluminum if applicable.
  • Grain Direction: Horizontal (standard), vertical, or diagonal
  • Letter Height: Overall height of the tallest character in the set
  • Thickness: Material thickness (typically 1/4" to 1/2" for most applications)
  • Font: Exact font name and weight, or vector artwork files
  • Mounting: Stud mount (specify standoff distance), adhesive mount, or flush mount

We accept artwork in Adobe Illustrator (.ai), EPS, or PDF vector format. If you only have raster artwork (JPG, PNG), we can vector-trace most logos and lettering for an additional artwork charge. Our standard production timeline is three weeks from approved artwork and finish sample approval. Rush schedules are available when timing is tight.

faqs.question: Can you ensure consistent grain direction across a large letter set?
faqs.answer: Yes. We use precision abrasive belt equipment that applies the brush pattern consistently across every letter. Grain direction (horizontal, vertical, or diagonal) is maintained uniformly across the entire order.

faqs.question: What is the difference between No. 4 and No. 3 brushed finishes?
faqs.answer: No. 4 is a standard satin finish with a medium grain that's most common in architectural signage. No. 3 is a coarser brush with more visible grain lines. We produce both, but No. 4 is our most frequently requested specification.

faqs.question: Will brushed aluminum letters show fingerprints?
faqs.answer: Brushed finishes are significantly more fingerprint-resistant than polished surfaces. Natural brushed aluminum shows fingerprints least, while anodized and clear-coated finishes hide them nearly completely. For high-touch environments, we recommend anodized brushed aluminum.

faqs.question: Can brushed metal letters be combined with painted elements?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. A popular combination is brushed metal lettering mounted on a painted aluminum backer panel. We produce both components and can supply them as a complete kit ready for installation.

title: Painted Flat Cut Letters | Wholesale | Sunlite
metaDescription: Wholesale painted flat cut letters with exact PMS and RAL color matching. Automotive-grade urethane finishes on aluminum or steel. 3-week German-engineered delivery.
heroSubtitle: Exact brand color matching on precision-cut dimensional letters. Automotive-grade polyurethane finishes that hold up to weather, UV exposure, and the test of time.

sections.heading: Precision Color Matching for Brand-Critical Projects
sections.content:
When a client's brand guidelines specify an exact color, painted flat cut letters are the way to deliver it. Unlike vinyl wraps or powder coat — which offer limited color-match precision — our automotive-grade polyurethane paint system achieves exact PMS, RAL, and custom color matches with batch-to-batch consistency that your brand-conscious clients demand.

Sunlite Signs uses a spectrophotometer-based color matching process that measures color in LAB color space, the same system used by major automotive and architectural coatings manufacturers. This eliminates the subjectivity of visual matching and ensures that the letters you install today will match letters you order six months from now for the same client.

Our paint booth environment is temperature and humidity controlled, which is essential for consistent color and finish quality. Letters are painted in a multi-stage process: zinc chromate primer for adhesion and corrosion resistance, base color coat applied in multiple light passes for uniform coverage, and a UV-stable clear coat for long-term finish protection. The result is a finish that resists fading, chalking, and peeling for a decade or more in exterior applications.

sections.heading: Substrate & Finish Specifications
sections.content:
Painted flat cut letters are available on the following substrates:

  • Aluminum (Standard): 6061-T6 alloy, CNC-routed with clean edges. The most popular substrate for painted letters due to its light weight and excellent paint adhesion.
  • Steel: Available in 16-gauge or 14-gauge mild steel for projects where the client wants maximum rigidity or a heavier feel.
  • Stainless Steel: For projects that require both a custom color and the corrosion resistance of stainless. Requires specialized primer for adhesion.

Finish options extend beyond solid colors to include:

  • Metallic Colors: Automotive metallic paints with fine or coarse metallic flake for a shimmering appearance
  • Matte, Satin, and Gloss: Full range of sheen levels from dead-flat matte to high-gloss mirror finish
  • Texture Finishes: Wrinkle, hammered, and sand textures applied through specialized spray techniques
  • Multi-Color: Two-tone or multi-color paint schemes with masked color breaks for complex brand applications

Every painted letter order can include a sprayed color sample chip for client approval before production begins. For color-critical national brand rollouts, we maintain paint formulas in our database for consistent matching across future orders.

sections.heading: Durability & Long-Term Performance
sections.content:
The longevity of painted flat cut letters depends entirely on the paint system. At Sunlite Signs, we use professional automotive-grade materials — not sign-industry shortcuts — because our trade partners need a finish that performs for years without callbacks or warranty claims.

Our multi-layer paint system delivers these performance characteristics:

  • Adhesion: Cross-hatch adhesion testing per ASTM D3359 on every production batch. Our chromate primer system achieves 5B (highest) adhesion ratings on aluminum substrates.
  • UV Resistance: ASTM G154 accelerated weathering tests demonstrate color retention exceeding 10 years of Florida exposure equivalent. The clear coat absorbs UV radiation before it reaches the color coat.
  • Chemical Resistance: The cured polyurethane finish resists common cleaning chemicals, bird droppings, tree sap, and urban pollution without staining or etching.
  • Impact Resistance: Flexible polyurethane formulation resists chipping from minor impacts during installation and in-service contact.

For projects in extreme UV environments (desert Southwest, high-altitude locations), we offer a PVDF (Kynar) topcoat upgrade that provides even greater long-term color stability. This coating is the same system used on architectural curtain wall panels that are warranted for 20+ years of exterior exposure.

faqs.question: How accurate is your PMS color matching?
faqs.answer: We use spectrophotometer-based matching that achieves Delta E values under 1.0 — within the threshold of visual color difference perception. For brand-critical projects, we provide sprayed samples for approval before production.

faqs.question: How long does the paint finish last on exterior letters?
faqs.answer: Our standard automotive-grade polyurethane system is tested to retain color and gloss for 10+ years in exterior applications. For extreme UV environments, our PVDF upgrade extends that to 20+ years of color stability.

faqs.question: Can you paint letters in multiple colors?
faqs.answer: Yes. We offer multi-color paint schemes using precision masking for clean color breaks. Two-tone lettering, gradient effects, and color-blocked designs are all achievable through our multi-stage masking and spraying process.

faqs.question: Do you keep paint formulas on file for reorders?
faqs.answer: Yes. We maintain a database of custom paint formulas indexed by client and project. This ensures that future orders match the original installation exactly, which is essential for multi-phase projects and national brand programs.

title: Polished Stainless Letters | Wholesale | Sunlite
metaDescription: Wholesale polished stainless steel letters with mirror-grade finish. Premium dimensional lettering for luxury environments. German-engineered, 3-week trade delivery.
heroSubtitle: Mirror-grade polished stainless steel dimensional letters that command attention through pure reflective brilliance. The ultimate statement in prestige signage.

sections.heading: Mirror-Polished Stainless: The Pinnacle of Dimensional Lettering
sections.content:
Mirror-polished stainless steel letters represent the highest expression of dimensional signage. The intensely reflective surface catches light and reflects the surrounding environment, creating a dynamic visual effect that changes with viewing angle, time of day, and ambient lighting. No other material or finish creates this level of visual impact.

Achieving a true mirror polish on stainless steel is a demanding process that requires patience, skill, and the right equipment. At Sunlite Signs, our polishing department uses a progressive multi-stage mechanical polishing process that takes each letter from raw machined surface through increasingly fine abrasive stages until a perfect mirror reflection is achieved. The final stage uses sub-micron polishing compound to eliminate even the finest scratches visible to the naked eye.

This level of finish quality is what separates a Sunlite mirror-polished letter from a competitor's "polished" product. Many shops stop at a semi-bright stage that looks polished at arm's length but reveals scratches and swirl marks under close inspection. Our German engineering standards demand a finish that holds up to scrutiny at any distance — because in a corporate lobby, people will be looking closely.

sections.heading: Ideal Applications for Mirror-Polished Letters
sections.content:
Mirror-polished stainless steel letters make the strongest impact in these environments:

  • Luxury Retail: High-end fashion, jewelry, and automotive brands use mirror-polished letters to communicate exclusivity and attention to detail
  • Corporate Headquarters: Fortune 500 lobby installations where the signage must match the prestige of the brand
  • Financial Institutions: Banks, investment firms, and insurance companies use polished stainless to project stability and success
  • Hotel Lobbies: Five-star and luxury boutique hotels where every design element contributes to the guest experience
  • Medical Centers: Premium healthcare facilities and specialty practices that want to communicate quality care from the entrance

For interior installations, mirror-polished letters interact beautifully with architectural lighting. Directed spotlights create bright highlights and dramatic shadows that make the letters appear to glow. Ambient lighting produces a subtler effect where the letters reflect the colors and textures of their surroundings, integrating seamlessly with the interior design.

Exterior mirror-polished installations are less common due to maintenance considerations, but can be spectacular in the right setting. We apply a nano-ceramic coating to exterior polished letters that adds water repellency and reduces fingerprint adhesion, making maintenance more manageable.

sections.heading: Care, Handling & Installation Best Practices
sections.content:
Mirror-polished stainless letters require more careful handling than brushed or painted alternatives. Sunlite Signs ships every polished letter with double-layer protective film, and we recommend the following best practices for your install crew:

  • Keep protective film in place until the letter is fully mounted and all surrounding construction work is complete
  • Wear clean cotton or microfiber gloves when handling polished surfaces — fingerprints can etch into the surface if left for extended periods
  • Remove protective film by pulling slowly at a sharp angle, not by yanking straight back
  • Clean installed letters with a microfiber cloth and stainless steel cleaner, wiping in the direction of any visible polish lines

Our mounting system for polished letters uses concealed threaded studs that are TIG-welded to the back of each letter. The welds are carefully controlled to prevent heat distortion on the polished face — a critical detail that many manufacturers overlook. We also include anti-rotation flats on each stud so the letter can't twist during tightening.

Standard lead time for mirror-polished stainless letters is three weeks from approved artwork. The polishing process is time-intensive, so rush orders may have limited availability. We recommend quoting polished stainless projects with adequate lead time built into the schedule.

faqs.question: How do you prevent heat distortion during stud welding?
faqs.answer: We use precisely controlled TIG welding with a copper heat sink behind the polished face. This draws heat away from the visible surface and prevents the warping or discoloration that can occur with uncontrolled welding on polished stainless.

faqs.question: Will mirror-polished letters show fingerprints?
faqs.answer: Yes, mirror-polished surfaces will show fingerprints. For high-touch environments, we apply an anti-fingerprint nano-coating that significantly reduces fingerprint visibility and makes cleaning easier. For lobby signs above touch height, this is generally not a concern.

faqs.question: Can mirror-polished letters be used outdoors?
faqs.answer: Yes, though they require more maintenance than brushed finishes to maintain their appearance. We apply a protective nano-ceramic coating for exterior installations that adds weather resistance. Regular cleaning with stainless steel cleaner will keep the mirror finish looking its best.

faqs.question: What grade of stainless steel do you polish?
faqs.answer: We use 304-grade stainless steel for polished letters. It accepts a mirror polish beautifully and provides excellent corrosion resistance for both interior and exterior applications. 316-grade is available for coastal projects.

title: Lobby Sign Letters | Wholesale | Sunlite Signs
metaDescription: Wholesale lobby sign letters in metal, acrylic, and mixed media. Precision-cut dimensional lettering for corporate and commercial interiors. 3-week trade delivery.
heroSubtitle: First impressions start in the lobby. Precision dimensional lettering in premium materials that elevate your client's brand from the moment visitors walk through the door.

sections.heading: Lobby Signage Sets the Tone for Every Visitor
sections.content:
The lobby is where first impressions are formed. Before a visitor meets a single employee, they've already assessed the organization based on the quality and professionalism of the reception area — and the lobby sign is the focal point of that assessment. A well-executed dimensional letter installation communicates that the organization pays attention to detail, values quality, and takes pride in its brand identity.

Sunlite Signs manufactures lobby sign letters for sign shops serving clients across every commercial sector. From Fortune 500 headquarters to local medical practices, the principles are the same: precision manufacturing, flawless finishes, and an installation system that ensures the letters go up perfectly every time.

Our German engineering standards are particularly evident in lobby sign work, where close viewing distances mean every detail is visible. Edge quality, finish uniformity, letter spacing, and mounting precision all matter at arm's length in a way they don't on a building exterior fifty feet above the street. We hold our lobby sign letters to the tightest tolerances in our production system because these are the letters people examine up close.

sections.heading: Material & Design Options for Lobby Letters
sections.content:
Lobby environments offer the most creative freedom in material selection because the letters are protected from weather and UV exposure. This opens up options that aren't practical for exterior use:

  • Brushed or Polished Metals: Aluminum, stainless steel, and brass in any surface finish. Interior installation means these finishes maintain their appearance indefinitely with minimal maintenance.
  • Painted Metals: Exact PMS color matching on aluminum or steel substrates. Interior environments are especially forgiving for painted finishes, as there's no UV or weather degradation.
  • Acrylic: Clear, colored, frosted, or metallic acrylic for modern, lightweight installations. Translucent acrylic enables dramatic backlit effects.
  • Mixed Media: Combine metal letters with acrylic backer panels, wood substrates, or fabric-wrapped backgrounds for layered, textured designs.

Mounting options for lobby signs include stud-mount with spacers for a floating effect, flush-mount directly to the wall, or mounted to a separate backer panel that's then hung on the wall. For glass-wall lobbies, we offer standoff-mount systems that attach through the glass with decorative hardware.

Our design support team can help you select materials and mounting methods that match the lobby's interior design direction. Send us photos of the space and your client's brand guidelines, and we'll provide material and finish recommendations.

faqs.question: What mounting method do you recommend for lobby signs on drywall?
faqs.answer: For drywall installations, we recommend stud-mount letters with toggle bolt anchors for letters over 1 lb each, or VHB adhesive mount for lighter letters. We include the appropriate hardware based on the letter weight and wall substrate you specify.

faqs.question: Can you mount lobby letters on glass walls?
faqs.answer: Yes. We offer standoff mounting hardware designed for through-glass installation. The hardware consists of decorative stainless steel standoffs with threaded studs that clamp through the glass. This creates a dramatic floating effect that's popular in modern lobby designs.

faqs.question: What letter height looks best in a typical lobby?
faqs.answer: For standard 9-10 foot ceiling lobbies, letter heights of 4" to 8" work well for most fonts. For double-height lobbies (18+ feet), letters of 10" to 18" are more appropriate. We can provide scaled mockup drawings to help visualize the proportions in the specific space.

faqs.question: Do you provide layout templates for lobby sign installation?
faqs.answer: Yes. Every lobby sign order includes a full-scale CNC-plotted paper template showing exact letter positions, spacing, and level reference lines. The template tapes directly to the wall, and the installer drills through the marked positions for precise placement.

faqs.question: Can lobby sign letters be backlit?
faqs.answer: Yes. We offer halo-lit lobby letters with LED strips mounted behind each letter that cast light onto the wall behind. We also offer face-lit options using translucent acrylic letters with LED modules inside. Both create stunning visual effects in controlled lobby lighting environments.

title: Office Flat Cut Letters | Wholesale | Sunlite
metaDescription: Wholesale office flat cut letters for suite signs, wayfinding, and tenant identification. Precision-cut in metal or acrylic. German-engineered, 3-week trade delivery.
heroSubtitle: Professional dimensional lettering for office suites, tenant identification, and commercial wayfinding. Consistent quality across multi-tenant programs.

sections.heading: Dimensional Lettering for Professional Office Environments
sections.content:
Office flat cut letters serve a practical purpose — identifying businesses and guiding visitors — while simultaneously reinforcing the professional image of the tenants and the property itself. In a competitive commercial real estate market, the quality of the signage program directly influences how tenants and their clients perceive the property's overall quality.

Sunlite Signs manufactures office flat cut letters for sign shops serving commercial property managers, tenant improvement contractors, and individual business clients. Our production system is optimized for both single-tenant custom projects and large-scale multi-tenant sign programs where consistency across dozens or hundreds of letter sets is essential.

For multi-tenant properties, we maintain material and finish specifications on file so that every new tenant sign matches the existing program exactly. Whether the original installation was done five months ago or five years ago, our spectrophotometer-matched paint system and cataloged material specifications ensure a seamless visual match.

sections.heading: Multi-Tenant Sign Programs & Consistency
sections.content:
Property management companies and commercial landlords need sign shops they can rely on for consistent quality across entire portfolios. Sunlite Signs supports this need through our multi-tenant sign program capabilities:

  • Standardized Specifications: We establish material, finish, letter height, font, and mounting specifications for each property and store them in our system for future reference.
  • Template Library: We maintain installation templates for each suite location, ensuring new tenant signs align with existing wayfinding and directional signage.
  • Volume Pricing: Multi-tenant programs receive volume pricing that remains stable for the duration of the property management contract.
  • Fast Turnaround: Because the specifications are already established, new tenant letter sets can move directly into production without the engineering phase. This reduces lead time for tenant changeovers.

Our German engineering approach to quality control means that letter set #50 in a program will be identical in quality to letter set #1. Material consistency, finish accuracy, and dimensional precision are monitored across every production run — not just spot-checked occasionally.

For sign shops managing multiple commercial properties, partnering with Sunlite for flat cut letter production means one reliable source for all your tenant signage needs, with the consistency and documentation that property managers demand.

sections.heading: ADA Compliance Considerations
sections.content:
While flat cut letters alone do not constitute ADA-compliant signage (ADA room signs require tactile characters and Grade 2 Braille), they are frequently used alongside ADA-compliant signs as part of a comprehensive office wayfinding program. Understanding the regulatory landscape helps you advise your clients correctly:

  • Overhead Signs: Flat cut letters mounted above doorways or at ceiling height are exempt from tactile requirements and can serve as secondary identification
  • Lobby & Feature Signs: Large-format decorative signage that is not the primary room identification sign is exempt from ADA tactile requirements
  • Directional Signs: Wayfinding signs that provide directional information but don't identify specific rooms have different requirements than room ID signs

Sunlite Signs produces both flat cut letters and ADA-compliant tactile signage, so we can supply your office projects with all the sign types needed for a complete, code-compliant wayfinding program. Contact our engineering team if you need guidance on which sign types require ADA compliance for your specific project.

faqs.question: Can you produce matching letter sets for an entire office building?
faqs.answer: Yes. We specialize in multi-tenant sign programs where every letter set must be consistent in material, finish, size, and mounting method. We store the program specifications in our system for seamless reorders when tenants change.

faqs.question: What font is most popular for office flat cut letters?
faqs.answer: Helvetica Neue, Gotham, and Futura are the most frequently specified fonts for office signage. However, we produce letters in any font — including custom brand typefaces — as long as vector artwork is provided.

faqs.question: Do office flat cut letters need to be ADA compliant?
faqs.answer: Flat cut letters used as decorative or secondary identification do not typically require ADA compliance. However, primary room identification signs must include tactile characters and Braille per ADA standards. We produce both types and can advise on your project's requirements.

faqs.question: How do you handle tenant changeovers in multi-tenant properties?
faqs.answer: We keep program specifications on file, so new tenant letter sets require only the new business name and suite number. Production bypasses the engineering phase and moves directly into manufacturing, reducing lead time to as little as two weeks.

title: Building Flat Cut Letters | Wholesale | Sunlite
metaDescription: Wholesale building flat cut letters for exterior facade identification. Large-format aluminum or stainless, engineered for wind load. German precision, 3-week delivery.
heroSubtitle: Large-format dimensional lettering engineered for building facades. Wind-load rated, corrosion-resistant, and built to maintain a commanding presence for decades.

sections.heading: Building Identification That Commands Attention
sections.content:
Building flat cut letters serve as the primary identification for commercial, institutional, and industrial properties. Mounted directly to the building facade, these large-format dimensional letters are visible from the street, from adjacent properties, and often from significant distances depending on letter height and building orientation. They're among the most permanent and prominent sign types a property owner will ever invest in.

At Sunlite Signs, we manufacture building flat cut letters from heavy-gauge aluminum or stainless steel plate engineered to withstand decades of exposure to weather, UV radiation, and wind loading. These are not lightweight interior letters scaled up for exterior use — they're purpose-built for the demands of facade mounting, with thicker material gauges, reinforced stud mounts, and finish systems rated for long-term exterior exposure.

Our engineering team analyzes every building letter project for wind-load compliance based on the installation location, letter size, mounting height, and local building code requirements. We provide mounting specifications that your install crew can follow with confidence, knowing the engineering has been thoroughly vetted.

sections.heading: Large-Format Engineering & Fabrication
sections.content:
Building-scale flat cut letters present engineering challenges that smaller letters don't. As letter height increases, material thickness must increase proportionally to prevent visible flexing in wind. Mounting hardware must handle greater weight and wind forces. And the finish system must resist UV degradation over a longer service life because replacing facade letters is a major-cost operation involving lifts and scaffolding.

Our fabrication approach for building flat cut letters includes:

  • Material Thickness: Minimum 3/8" aluminum or 1/4" stainless steel for letters 12" to 24" tall. Letters 24" to 48" use 1/2" to 3/4" plate. Letters over 48" may use 1" plate or built-up fabrication.
  • Mounting System: Heavy-gauge threaded rod studs welded to the back of each letter, typically 3/8" or 1/2" diameter depending on letter weight and wind-load requirements.
  • Finish System: PVDF (Kynar) coatings standard on exterior building letters for 20+ year color retention. High-build primer system for maximum corrosion protection.
  • Wind-Load Analysis: Engineering calculations per ASCE 7 for the specific installation location, including exposure category, wind speed, and height factor.

For letters taller than our CNC router bed (48"), we fabricate in sections with precision-aligned splice joints that are virtually invisible once installed. The splice connections are engineered to maintain structural continuity across the joint.

faqs.question: What is the maximum size for building flat cut letters?
faqs.answer: There is no practical maximum size. Letters up to 48" are cut from a single plate. Larger letters are fabricated in sections with concealed splice joints. We've produced building letters up to 96" tall using this sectional approach.

faqs.question: Do building flat cut letters require engineering stamps?
faqs.answer: Many jurisdictions require stamped engineering for facade-mounted signs, especially at significant heights. We provide stamped structural engineering drawings as an available service, covering letter mounting, wind-load resistance, and fastener specifications.

faqs.question: What finish is best for exterior building letters?
faqs.answer: We recommend PVDF (Kynar) coatings for exterior building letters. This finish system provides 20+ years of color retention and is the same coating used on architectural curtain walls. It's significantly more durable than standard polyurethane paint for long-term exterior exposure.

faqs.question: How are large building letters installed?
faqs.answer: Large building letters are typically installed using aerial lifts or scaffolding. We provide a paper or digital template that's applied to the building face, drill holes are marked through the template, threaded studs on the letter backs are inserted into the holes, and letters are secured from behind.

faqs.question: Can building flat cut letters be halo-lit?
faqs.answer: Yes. We offer halo-lit building letters with LED strip lighting mounted behind each letter on standoff spacers. The light washes the building facade behind the letter, creating a dramatic nighttime presence. All halo-lit letters carry UL 48 certification.
